observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances through the issuance of demand letters to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Also, conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis on the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability. Management is encouraged to request write-off on dormant receivable accounts by submitting duly certified relevant documents.

We recommended that the LGU shall take advantage of the latest technology by adopting the system of payroll payment through the automated teller machine of Landbank of the Philippines (LBP)-Mati City, Davao Oriental, its depository bank, in order to strengthen the control on cash management.
In addition, the management shall consider securing the municipal office, where the ATM is proposed to be installed, by employing security guards within the office ground in order to address potential threats and risks of robbery and break-ins.

We recommended that management shall submit immediately the reported lacking vouchers to preclude the issuance of Notices of Suspension. In addition, the management shall identify the breakdown in the internal control and, if necessary, shall impose disciplinary action on personnel identified who are not performing their duties and responsibilities.  Also, personnel concerned shall strictly observe and submit the required financial and other reports within the prescribed deadlines to effect timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy, otherwise, the penal provision under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 shall be applied which state that:
“Failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.” (Emphasis ours)

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Consider establishing a General Service Office and/or designate a full-time property custodian who will oversee a workable inventory system for the LGU to ascertain that accountability over these government assets is likewise prioritized.
Require the personnel-in-charge for the submission of pertinent documents and reports on the utilization and consumption of the inventories and impose appropriate disciplinary action in the event that the personnel cannot submit the same within the deadline set by the Accounting Office.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile any accounting records against the inventory records maintained by the Municipal Treasurer (in the absence of GSO) and undertake the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the result of reconciliation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Consider establishing a General Service Office and/or designate a full-time property custodian officer who will oversee a workable inventory system for the LGU to ascertain that accountability over these government assets is likewise prioritized.
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory count of the materials and supplies to determine the unconsumed/unissued/undistributed portion of the expenses which will be the basis for the Municipal Accountant to record the related inventory accounts.  It should also be noted that Section 124, Volume I, Chapter 7 of the NGAS prescribes:
“Section 124. Inventory of Supplies and Materials. The local chief executive shall require periodic physical inventory of supplies or property.  Physical count of inventory items by type shall be conducted semestrally and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).  This shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
Management’s Comments:
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant acknowledged their lapses in erroneously recording some transactions under the Office Supplies Expenses account, however, he also explained that he already reclassified those transactions to their proper asset accounts.
Moreover, he further justified that the absence of a General Service Office and the lack of personnel hampered the inventory taking of these supplies at year-end.  In their reply letter, the management is very much serious in gearing towards establishing a General Service Office that will oversee the inventory activities of the LGU once the appropriation for CY 2022 has already been enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan.  They are already in the move of scouting for a full-time property custodian from the pool of permanent employees.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Direct the official/personnel concerned to comply with the prescribed guidelines set under COA Circular No. 2018-002 in order to insure the properties and supplies of the LGU and to conform to RA No. 656, as amended by PD No. 245; and
Request the Sangguniang Bayan to provide a supplemental budget for the application of insurance for the properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and scrutinize the supporting documents from the program of works whether the transactions can be considered capitalizable or not prior to recording the road projects as a PPE item.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review the prior years’ recording of the transactions under Road Networks while identifying those transactions that do not qualify as Property, Plant and Equipment and prepare adjusting entries to correct the improper recording, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Request the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate budget and allocate the necessary funds for the payment of processing fees that may be incurred in the titling thereof.
Direct the Chairman of the Inventory Committee to expedite the transfer of ownership of the aforesaid parcels of land in the name of the LGU to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO-Designate of any available data pertaining to the PPE accounts cited above and reconcile their records particularly the date of acquisition of PPEs; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the proper depreciation of the PPE items through the preparation of lapsing schedule or any equivalent schedule and to subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entries recognizing the accumulated depreciation of these assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Consider establishing a General Service Office and/or designate a full-time property officer who will oversee a workable inventory system for the LGU to ascertain that accountability over these government assets is likewise prioritized.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the PPELC or subsidiary ledger cards of all recorded PPE in the books of accounts apart from posting these PPE items in the subsidiary ledgers.
Direct the GSO-Designate to delegate to a personnel-in-charge under her supervision, and not to the Chairman of the Inventory Committee, the preparation and maintenance of the property cards of all the PPEs owned by the LGU to account for the receipt and disposition of these PPEs to avoid designating incompatible duties.  Likewise, secure a seminar training for the said personnel-in-charge to acquire basic knowledge of property and supply management.
Instruct the GSO-Designate to establish the property records that should be maintained by the property officer and refrain from obtaining records from the accounting office to observe a proper check and balance of the PPE items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Create an Inventory Committee who shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time to the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed in three (3) months or less as required under Sections 5.2 and 5.3 of the said COA Circular.
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), have it approved by the Municipal Mayor, and submit the approved PIP to the Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory taking activities.
Ensure the full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the implementing departments, shall monitor the utilization of funds from the national government agencies and immediately cause the return of the unutilized balance to the SA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
As head of the agency, to push for inclusion among the priority PPAs those that are truly responsive to the needs of the community through conducting current reality dialogues with the concerned officials (e.g. Barangay Officials) to gain insights and information on the overall development situation in the community.
Ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% development fund in accordance with the revised guidelines to attain the desired outcomes for the benefit of the constituents of Manay.
Direct the department heads concerned to prioritize the implementation of the remaining 3 projects during the 1st quarter of the year, likewise, to the proposed developmental projects intended for CY 2022.

We recommended that the LDRRMO shall prepare the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of MDRRMF to be certified by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor of the LGU.
Moreover, adhere strictly to the pertinent laws, rules and regulations specifically on the implementation and utilization of MDRRM funds.

We recommended that all purchases of office supplies commonly used in the LGU’s operations shall be made thru the PS-DBM as required by law.
In cases where the office supplies from PS-DBM are not available, the certification issued by the PS-DBM indicating the non-availability of stocks shall be the basis for the BAC to recommend the use of alternative modes of procurement subject to the approval of the head of the procuring entity.
Moreover, the BAC shall explore using online technology in obtaining CNAS from the PS-DBM to save time and effort in traveling to its Regional Office to secure one.

We recommended that all claims/payments against government funds are adequately supported with required documents pursuant to Section 2 (2) of BIR RR No. 18-2012.  Consequently, request for issuance of valid sales invoice of the above-noted payments to support the transaction, otherwise, issuance of a Notice of Suspension may be deemed necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Investigate the incident and direct the personnel-in-charge to locate the records and documents previously stored at the Office of the Auditor.
Remind the management to, henceforth, refrain from the unauthorized transfer of records under COA custody and secure proper authority/permission when the need to transfer the same arises.
Impose sanctions to responsible officials and/or employees when warranted as provided in 124 of RA 9470, which states:
“Any public officer committing any unlawful acts or omissions mandated under this act shall be punished by a fine of not less than Five Hundred Thousand Pesos (P500,000.00) but not exceeding One Million Pesos (P1,000,000.00) or be imprisoned for not less than five (5) years but not more than fifteen (15) years and/or bot fine and imprisonment at the discretion of the court without prejudice to the filing of administrative charges that would result to such public officer’s perpetual disqualification from public office, and forfeiture, in favor of the government, of his salary and other lawful income.”

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to observe the deadline set in Section 9 of RA 5447 on the remittance of provincial shares accruing to the SEF so that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs, and activities beneficial to the constituents of the province of Davao Oriental.
Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant shall observe close coordination in the submission of documents to avoid any delays in the remittance of the RPT share.

We recommended that management shall direct the Property/Supply Officer/LGU Inspector to accomplish the pre-repair inspection report after the conduct of pre-repair inspection of properties subjected for repairs and furnished a copy of this report to the COA Auditor within five (5) days from the date of evaluation/inspection.
Likewise, immediately conduct a post-repair inspection and submit a report thereon when the properties were repaired.
Also, prepare the Waste Materials Report for all spare parts that need replacement and are subject for disposal.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that management enforce the  immediate  liquidation  of
the outstanding cash advances through the issuance of letters of  demand  to
officials and employees concerned, and strictly comply with  the  provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and  liquidation  of
cash advances.


      Also, conduct regular and periodic verification and  analysis  on  the
existence of receivables and unliquidated cash  advances  to  determine  the
extent of collectability. Management is encouraged to  make  a  request  for
write-off on  dormant  receivable  accounts  by  submitting  duly  certified
relevant documents.

Recommendations:


      We recommended the following:


   1.  For  the  Municipal  Treasurer,  expedite  the  preparation  of   the
      Treasurer’s certified list  of  taxpayers  showing  the  name  of  the
      taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which is  to
      be used by the Municipal Accountant in the setting-up of  the  correct
      amounts of the RPT/SET Receivables.


   2. For the Municipal Accountant, prepare the necessary adjusting  entries
      to correct the erroneous recording of the RPT/SET Receivable  accounts
      once the RPTAR/TIC has already been provided.

Recommendations:

    We  recommended  that  the  designated  Chairperson  of  the  Inventory
Committee shall ensure the conduct of the physical count of  all  properties
of the municipality. Thereafter, prepare and submit the  RPCPPE  within  the
prescribed period, showing the corresponding remarks  on  the  condition  of
the listed equipment and reconcile with the accounting records to  determine
unrecorded  properties.  Unserviceable  items  shall  be  disposed   of   in
accordance with the above-mentioned manual. Also,  keep  and  update  ledger
cards and property cards to facilitate the reconciliation of physical  count
and accounting/property records.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

   1. Identify the cause of over disbursement of the transactions identified
      under the Due to LGUs  account  and  establish  the  validity  of  the
      disbursements made.


   2. Notify and demand payment from the supplier  relative  to  the  double
      claim  of  retention  fee,  otherwise,  the  issuance  of  Notice   of
      Disallowance may be deemed necessary.

   3. Analyze the accounts of those payables with negative balances and make
      the necessary adjustments to  reflect  the  correct  balances  of  the
      payables and set-up a  receivable  account  for  the  double-claim  of
      guaranty/security deposits payable.

Recommendations:

      We recommend that Management:

   1. Require the Municipal  Accountant  and  the  responsible  offices,  as
      mentioned in Table 1,  to  submit  immediately  the  reported  lacking
      vouchers, financial records and other reports  within  the  prescribed
      period to effect timely review and evaluation of the  transactions  as
      to their propriety, completeness and  accuracy  and  to  preclude  the
      issuance of Notices of Suspension on these transactions.

   2. Adopt measures that would ensure the submission of financial  accounts
      and reports within  the  prescribed  period.   And  in  the  event  of
      repeated and unjustified failure of the accountable officers concerned
      to submit these accounts and reports, impose the applicable  sanctions
      as provided in Section 122 (2) of PD 1445 which states that –


           “Failure on the part of the officials concerned  to  submit  the
           documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause
           the suspension of payment of their  salaries  until  they  shall
           have  complied  with  the  requirements  of   the   Commission.”
           (Emphasis ours)

We recommended the timely preparation and immediate submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts including paid/cancelled checks, debit/credit memo and bank statements to make possible the verification of the accuracy of the Cash-in-Bank balances on the financial statements during the year.

possible the verification of the accuracy of the  Cash-in-Bank  balances  on

count of inventories, submit the required reports and  maintain  ledger  and
stock cards.

Office to fairly present the actual financial position  and  performance  of
the municipality.

municipality.  Thereafter,  prepare  and  submit  the  RPCPPE   within   the

unrecorded  properties.  Unserviceable  items  shall  be  disposed   of   in
accordance with the above-mentioned manual.

reconciliation of physical count and accounting records.

of PD 1445 shall be applied.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  shall  monitor  the
utilization of the funds transferred from national government  agencies  and
immediately cause the return of the unutilized balance  to  the  SA,  unless
otherwise the Memorandum of Agreement so provides.

      We further recommended that funds received  from  private  and  public
monies which have officially come into the possession  of  the  municipality
for the  fulfilment  of  a  specific  purpose  shall  be  deposited  in  the
appropriate bank account/s under Trust Fund.

We recommended that expenses which are administrative in nature shall  be
charged against the regular budget of current operating expenditures  of
the municipality.  Likewise,   concerned   personnel   shall   ensure   complete   and   proper
documentation of all claims against government funds. Correct  recording  of
transactions shall also be observed.

We recommended strengthening the workforce  of  the  MDRRM  office  by
appropriating the budgetary requirements for the three  mandatory  positions
and current operating expenditures of the office. Also,  ensure  the  prompt
submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of  DRRMF,  MDRRMFIP
and any subsequent revisions thereto.

We recommended that management shall adhere strictly to the time frame
in  which  the  ESWMP  should  be  implemented   and   to   facilitate   the
establishment of MRFs in every barangay or  cluster  of  barangays  for  the
welfare of the entire populace of the municipality.

We recommended that management shall direct the personnel concerned to
submit the identified lacking supporting  documents  of  the  abovementioned
disbursements. Also, written justifications and additional  documents  shall
be submitted to prove that the teachers were part of  the  RQA  of  Teachers
and that the school  deployments  were  identified  were  among  those  with
shortage of teachers by the DepEd during the school year they were hired.

We recommended management to expedite  the  utilization  of  the  fund
throughout the year and to implement the PPAs which will contribute  to  the
prevention of juvenile delinquency and promotion of the rights  and  welfare
of children.  Likewise,  require  the  concerned  personnel  to  submit  the
aforementioned reports and disbursement vouchers for November and  December,
if any, to enable us to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that the designated GSO shall  ensure  the  conduct  of
physical count of  all  the  properties  of  the  municipality.  Thereafter,
prepare the report with the corresponding remarks on the  condition  of  the
listed equipment and reconcile with  the  accounting  records  to  determine
unrecorded properties. Also, keep  and  update  ledger  cards  and  property
cards   to    facilitate    reconciliation    of    physical    count    and
accounting/property records.

We recommended that the accountant or  his  authorized  representative
shall submit the cash advance vouchers  with  covering  transmittal  letters
and supporting documents within 7 days after release of check. Also,  submit
a copy of the Confirmation Reply from  National  Treasury  of  the  approved
bond of  the  disbursing  officer  with  coverage  applicable  to  the  cash
advances granted.

We  recommended  that  management  shall  facilitate   the   immediate
relocation of the resident in order to start the construction.  We  have
further  recommended  the  strict  monitoring  of  the  progress   of   the
construction in order to prevent incurring delay in the  implementation  of
the project.

We recommended that the MPDC shall properly identify and  address  the
causes of non-implementation of the projects to  facilitate  the  attainment
of the benefits of the PPAs that can be derived therefrom.
Remit the aforesaid PDAF balances to the Bureau of Treasury and submit the validated deposit slip for our reference.
